title: Fractional CFO
description:
Freelance and Contract Financial Controllers

**You set your pricing. You choose your clients. You control your work.**

Elite Fractional Collective exists to support experienced fractional professionals with a clearer, more stable way to be seen, while keeping full ownership of their businesses and client relationships. The Collective provides the professional a pathway to connect with clients while maintaining their independence, autonomy in their client relationships and setting their own pricing. Not only do we provide the support to your pipeline, the goal is to create a community of like minded individuals to lean in on for shared resources and knowledge. We are becoming a trusted place for businesses to find experienced fractional professionals.

This is a membership opportunity to fill your pipeline, please check us out at

**www.elitefractionalcollective.com**

A Controller is a senior\-level manager who oversees a company's daily accounting operations, financial reporting, and internal controls, acting as the "historian" of financial data while also providing analysis for future strategy, often reporting to the CFO. They manage teams, budgets, payroll, and compliance, ensuring accurate financial records and providing insights to help leadership make informed decisions.

Work Location: Remote

title: Fractional CFO
description:
Fractional CFO / Cash Flow Advisor

Company: FantAutistic Skills Training (FAST)

Type: Part\-Time / Contract (5–15 hours per month)

Industry: Healthcare / ABA Therapy

Role Overview

FAST is a growing ABA therapy provider seeking a Fractional CFO or Cash Flow Advisor to oversee financial stability and cash flow planning. The primary responsibility of this role is to ensure the company maintains sustainable operations while navigating insurance reimbursement cycles and rapid client growth.

This position focuses on cash flow forecasting, payroll sustainability, and financial planning, working alongside our CPA and billing team.

Key Responsibilities

Cash Flow Management

* Build and maintain a 12\-week rolling cash flow forecast
* Monitor upcoming payroll obligations and ensure adequate liquidity
* Identify potential cash gaps before they occur

Financial Oversight

* Analyze revenue vs payroll sustainability
* Evaluate staffing levels relative to billable hours
* Monitor financial health of the organization

Insurance Revenue Analysis

* Work with the billing team to track:

\*

* claims submitted
* expected reimbursements
* payment timelines

\*

* Forecast incoming insurance payments

Financial Planning

* Develop a monthly financial model for the company
* Advise on safe hiring and staffing expansion
* Recommend strategies for building operating reserves

Strategic Advisory

* Provide leadership with guidance on:

\*

* financing decisions
* operational sustainability
* growth planning

\*

Collaboration

The Fractional CFO will work directly with:

* Founder / CEO
* CPA (tax and accounting)
* ABA billing team

The role is focused on coordinating financial information and providing strategic oversight, not performing bookkeeping or tax preparation.

Ideal Qualifications

* Experience as a CFO, Controller, or financial advisor
* Experience working with healthcare, therapy clinics, or behavioral health companies
* Strong experience with cash flow forecasting and financial modeling
* Familiarity with insurance reimbursement cycles is strongly preferred

Engagement Structure

* Contract / Fractional position
* Estimated 5–15 hours per month
* Flexible schedule
* Remote work acceptable

Primary Goal

The primary objective of this role is to ensure that FAST maintains financial stability while scaling operations, particularly by managing cash flow during periods of rapid client growth.

Job Type: Part\-time

Work Location: Remote

title: Fractional CFO, Controller or Analyst
description:
IMPORTANT: PLEASE DON'T APPLY IF YOU'RE NOT WILLING TO DO THE VIDEO INTERVIEW FOR US. OUR ENTIRE BUSINESS IS ON VIDEO, AND PRESENTATION MATTERS! We are aware that applying for jobs is not easy so we don't want to burn your time. All of our team members perform their jobs on video during a zoom meeting. Video is required to maintain that personal touch with the clients. Advice, have a nice background with decent lighting.

**Who are we:** We are business engineers who deconstruct business data and solve problems to increase profitability! AKA: Fractional CFO’s

**How do we do it:** We connect to all business data the client has available, e.g: Facebook, (other marketing products), QBO, SAS systems, Point of Sale systems, bank accounts, etc., and manipulate that data into our (Integrated Financial Model / Forecast) proprietary software.

**What is our Objective:** Show our clients their business in a way they have never been able to see it. Figure out what's in their way of growth. Find missing / leaking funds and advice to stop the burn. You will also be coaching the client on areas of the business that needs changes according to the IFM.

**What software must I be familiar with:** You must be proficient with Google Docs, especially Google Sheets and be able to manipulate, and create proper formulas within sheets. QBO is a must, and basic understanding of how software will connect with others.

**Who are we looking to hire:** Are you an experienced and qualified CFO, Controller or financial business coach who is looking for a flexible and rewarding career? Would you enjoy having your own book of business that you focus on? Do you enjoy working with different clients and industries and helping them achieve their financial goals? If so, you might be the perfect fit for our fractional CFO firm.

**Where will I report to work:** You will work from the comfort of your own home. Your meetings will be held on Zoom with video on.

**About us:** Weekly Accounting is growing quickly due to our dedication to fractional CFO services for startups and small businesses across the US. We grew 100% in 2025 and need to fill a few more positions. We offer our clients access to high\-quality CFOs who can handle various financial tasks and challenges on a full time basis. Our fractional CFOs are employed directly with our company

**How much can I make:** We expect our CFO's to earn over 6 figures fairly quickly.

**What is the compensation:** Our average client pays us $1,500 \- $2,000k a month for the build out of the IFM and the 2 half hour coaching sessions per month. You will get paid on average 20% of the billable. This equates to approximately $300\. \- $400 per client to hold an average of 2\-hr worth of meetings per month.

**Team:** You will also have a team around you to help support the clients. Build the actual Integrated Financial Model and help assist with the client. We have executive assistants and model builders working the client data to better support you in the coaching process along with highly trained bookkeepers.

**As a fractional CFO, you will be responsible for:**

* Providing strategic financial guidance and analysis to your clients via our IFM (Integrated Financial Model) which includes items such as Unit Economics, financial projections, budgets, cash flow statements, and financial reports.
* Manipulating our Financial Modeling and Forecasting custom platform
* Establishing and ADVISING on implementation of financial policies, procedures, and controls that ensure the integrity and efficiency of your clients' financial operations.
* Communicating and maintaining relationships with your clients and stakeholders.

**Who gets hired for the position? Do you meet some of these qualifications?**

* At least 4 years of experience as a CFO, Fractional CFO, Controller, or a senior financial executive in a variety of industries and sectors. If you have extensive experience in Financial Modeling, we will overlook the time requirements.
* A strong knowledge of accounting standards and reporting requirements.
* A proficient use of bookkeeping or accounting software, such as QuickBooks, Xero, or FreshBooks. Bonus \- If you have Netsuite or Sage experience, we really want to talk to you!
* An excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills.
* A high level of professionalism, integrity, and ethics.
* A self\-motivated, proactive, and adaptable attitude.
* A willingness to learn new skills and technologies and stay updated on the latest financial trends and best practices.

If you are interested in joining our fractional CFO firm, please send us your resume and a cover letter explaining why you are the best candidate for the role. We will quickly review your resume and cover letter, then send you a request for a recorded video Q\&A which leads to the full interview.

Pay: $60,000\.00 \- $200,000\.00 per year

Work Location: Remote

title: Fractional Chief Financial Officer
description:
Valentis Group, Inc., a nationally recognized private security firm, seeks a fractional CFO who provides strategic financial leadership.


**JOB OVERVIEW**  

We are seeking a skilled Fractional Chief Financial Officer to join our team on a part\-time basis. The Fractional CFO will be responsible for overseeing all financial activities, providing strategic financial guidance, and supporting the overall financial health of the organization.  

  

**QUALIFICATIONS**


* Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field; MBA or CPA preferred
* Proven experience as a CFO or in a senior financial management position
* Strong knowledge of financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting
* Excellent leadership and communication skills
* Ability to collaborate with executives and lead a team


**RESPONSIBILITIES**


* Develop financial strategies in line with the company's goals and objectives
* Work alongside current bookkeeping firm
* Manage the financial planning and analysis process
* Oversee financial reporting and compliance
* Provide insights and recommendations to improve financial performance
* Guide financial decision\-making and strategic planning
* Collaborate with executive management to drive financial success


**ADDITIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES**


* Part\-time availability to fulfill the role of Fractional CFO
* Experience working in a fractional or consulting capacity is a plus
* Knowledge of financial software and systems
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills

title: Controller and Account Manager
description:
**Controller**

*Client Advisory \& Team Leadership Focus*

**Location:** Hybrid – North Loop, Minneapolis, MN (Twin Cities Metro Area)

**Summary**

Join **Owl Bookkeeping and CFO Services** as a **hands\-on Controller** who thrives on variety, challenge, and impact. Partner with entrepreneurs, lead accounting excellence, and make a meaningful difference for growing businesses.

**About the Role**

Are you a seasoned **Controller** who thrives in a **collaborative, challenging, and rewarding environment**—where every day brings new opportunities to solve problems, support business owners, and make an impact?

At **Owl Bookkeeping and CFO Services**, we’re looking for a **hands\-on Controller** to deliver high\-level financial insight and accounting leadership across multiple client engagements. In some partnerships, you’ll collaborate with a bookkeeper who supports day\-to\-day transactions; in others, you’ll handle the full controller scope yourself.

This position is ideal for someone who enjoys **diving into complex financials, improving processes, and providing trusted strategic guidance** to business owners. You’ll work directly with entrepreneurs to deliver clear, accurate financials and actionable insights that drive growth.

This role is perfect for a **self\-starter** who enjoys variety, autonomy, and meaningful client relationships.

**What You’ll Do**

* Lead **accounting processes** for multiple clients, ensuring **accuracy, timeliness, and insight.**
* Collaborate with accounting staff to deliver quality financials and process improvements.
* Prepare **budgets, forecasts, and performance analyses.**
* Advise clients with **strategic, data\-driven recommendations.**
* Support **documentation, workflow improvements, and system optimization.**

**What You Bring**

* Proven experience providing **controller\-level financial oversight.**
* Strong **QuickBooks Online and Desktop proficiency.**
* Ability to manage **multiple clients and priorities** with precision.
* Excellent **communication** and **relationship\-building** skills.
* Commitment to **accuracy, accountability, and continuous improvement.**
* Experience mentoring or collaborating with **bookkeepers or accounting staff.**
* **Knowledge of GAAP** and financial reporting best practices.

**Why You’ll Love Working at Owl**

* **Hybrid flexibility** – work from our North Loop office and onsite with local clients.
* **Supportive, growth\-oriented team culture.**
* **Career development opportunities** in client strategy and leadership.
* **Paid parking, free breakfast and snacks, onsite gym, 401(k) match, tiered PTO, and generous benefits.**

If you’re looking for a place where your expertise matters — where you can **mentor others, advise clients, and shape the financial health of growing businesses** — apply today!

Pay: $90,000\.00 \- $120,000\.00 per year

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Disability insurance
* Health insurance
* Health savings account

Application Question(s):

* Have you worked in an outsourced, client\-service, or fractional accounting environment before?
* Are you able to work in a hybrid role based in the Twin Cities metro area (North loop office and local client visits)? This is not remote work. Applicants need to live in the Twin Cities and spend time between our co\-working space and client sites.
* How comfortable are you managing multiple clients or entities simultaneously? Which task management tools do you use to help you?
* Describe your experience managing or mentoring accounting or bookkeeping staff. How do you hold staff accountable to objectives?

* Controller or Senior Accounting: 10 years (Required)

title: Interim CFO
description:
**Interim Chief Financial Officer**  

**Ascent CFO Solutions**


At Ascent CFO Solutions, we are a leading Fractional CFO services firm dedicated to providing strategic financial leadership and insights to startups and SMEs. Our mission is to empower businesses to achieve financial clarity and sustainable growth. As we continue to expand our services and client base, we are seeking experienced Interim Chief Financial Officers (CFOs) to join our dynamic team of fractional financial professionals.


In this full\-time (\~40 hours/week) contractor role, you will serve as a trusted advisor to a diverse portfolio of clients navigating transformation, rapid growth, operational improvements, fundraising, M\&A, and exit preparation. This is a hands\-on, entrepreneurial opportunity for a finance leader who thrives in fast\-paced environments and enjoys working across industries.

### **Position Overview**


As an Interim CFO, you will step into organizations during critical transition periods—whether covering a leave of absence, filling a gap between permanent CFOs, supporting a restructuring, or stabilizing the finance function during rapid growth. You will provide immediate executive\-level financial leadership, ensuring continuity, stability, and strategic direction while maintaining business momentum. You will be accountable for client satisfaction, deliverable accuracy, timeliness, and overall engagement success.


Our clients include startups, scale\-ups, privately held companies, and VC\- and PE\-backed organizations across a wide range of industries such as Technology, SaaS, Professional Services, Construction, Manufacturing, eCommerce, Healthcare, CPG, FinTech, Real Estate, and more.

### **Key Responsibilities**

* Serve as a strategic partner and “right hand” to CEOs and executive teams
* Develop and execute comprehensive financial strategies aligned with business goals
* Build and maintain financial models, rolling forecasts, and cash flow projections
* Lead budgeting processes and analyze budget\-to\-actual performance
* Identify and monitor key financial trends, KPIs, and business metrics
* Develop executive dashboards and board\-ready reporting packages
* Support and lead fundraising efforts, including venture capital and private equity transactions
* Lead M\&A initiatives, including due diligence, valuation support, and post\-acquisition integration
* Oversee or directly manage month\-end close processes and financial reporting
* Assess and improve accounting processes, systems, and internal controls
* Coordinate annual audits and manage tax return processes
* Present financial results and strategic insights to Boards of Directors and shareholders
* Lead client onboarding engagements, including financial assessments and transition planning

### **Ideal Candidate Profile**

* 15\+ years of progressive accounting and finance experience
* Several years serving as a full\-time CFO
* Bachelor’s degree required; CPA license and/or MBA strongly preferred
* Demonstrated experience as a strategic financial leader and executive partner
* Proven success working with fast\-growth companies
* Extensive experience building financial forecasts and cash flow models
* Strong background in M\&A, including due diligence and integration
* Experience raising capital, particularly within the venture capital ecosystem
* Comfortable operating in both strategic and hands\-on capacities
* Experience working across multiple industries
* Strong ERP and accounting systems experience (QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, Sage Intacct, and similar platforms)
* Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel; Google Sheets experience a plus
* Entrepreneurial mindset with exceptional communication and leadership skills

### **Location**


Location may be fully remote or on\-site depending on the client’s request

title: Controller Advisor
description:
About Level10 CFO

Level10 CFO offers a fresh take on outsourced CFO advisory and accounting services. We lead with an advisory\-first approach on all client engagements. That could mean assisting a start\-up company needing bookkeeping and some help with prospective financials for an investor deck. It could also mean working with a growth middle\-market company needing a CFO to help with strategic financial planning and a controller to bring controls and efficiency to its transactional accounting environment. Though we promote the use of EOS® (Entrepreneurial Operating System… ask us later!), we don’t demand it of our clients. Its constructs are woven into how we engage and drive value for our clients. We live these core values:

* Our clients’ interests always come first.
* We brave the crucial conversations.
* We embrace technology.
* We actively challenge the status quo.
* We like to laugh (even at ourselves!).

Summary

The Controller Advisor must present as a self\-assured (but not cocky) consultant to the organization who can quickly establish credibility, trust, and rapport, both with leadership and with any staff under direction. The Controller should be dogged in making sure the trains run on time, relentless in driving efficiency through process, and make a Supermax prison warden envious of the control environment. Each day should start with the question, “How can I leave the client better off than I found them?”

The Controller Advisor is also expected to contribute to the evolution and growth of Level10 CFO, including but not limited to our process, procedure, technical maturity, and general enjoyment of coming to work each day.

Experience

* Been to a few rodeos. Enjoyed a couple rides up and down the business roller coaster. Have the scars to prove it.
* Been the right\-hand man/woman, the consigliere to the CFO.
* Been responsible for all aspects of the transactional accounting environment and the publishing of (accurate and timely!) financial statements.
* Mix of start\-up and mid\-market Controller, Accounting Manager, or like senior accounting work history is ideal.
* Systems and technology implementation experience.
* Extra credit:
* Finance, Planning, and Analysis (FP\&A) experience.
* Experience in technology/software and/or services organizations, especially agency model businesses.
* Capital raising and M\&A experience.
* Experience in consulting and/or fractional environments.

Skills

* Diplomacy and communication.
* Proven ability to lead, manage, and hold staff accountable, and to coordinate work effectively and push work to the right level of a human capital stack.
* Efficient, clear, and concise process creation and documentation ability.
* Ability (and excitement!) to adopt and maximize use of new technologies and drive adoption in the organization.
* Brown belt in Excel/Google Sheets. Black belt bonus!
* Disassemble and reassemble a flux capacitor.
* P.S. Knowing GAAP and preparing financial statements are table stakes.

Education

* BA/BS Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related major.
* CPA certification preferred.
* If you have a Masters, we won’t hold it against you.

Working Conditions

* This position is a fully remote position. Occasional weekend and/or evening work may be required.

Physical components

* Must be able to bench press 225 for reps and deadlift 2x own body weight. Ok, maybe just lift a printer or similar office equipment.

Work Remotely

* Yes

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $80,000\.00 \- $120,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Remote

description:

**SPECIAL INSTRUCTIONS TO APPLICANT:**  

Each applicant is required to provide (as attachments to the online application) the following supplemental documents: (1\) a letter providing some detail of the applicant's qualifications and interest in the position; and (2\) current resume/CV. \- PLEASE NOTE \- during the application process, you will be prompted to attach these documents in the area titled "Resume." Please either combine ALL documents into ONE attachment, OR upload each item separately in this section. Failure to upload ALL of the required documents may disqualify application from consideration.
  
For inquiries and additional information, please contact Human Resource Services via email at hrs@sou.edu or by phone at (541\)552\-8553\.
  
To view SOU's very generous benefits and pension programs available to eligible positions, please visit https://inside.sou.edu/assets/hrs/Benefits/Benefit\_Flyer/2024\_Prospective\_Candidate\_Benefit\_Overview\_012024\.pdf
  
Our benefits package is an important complement to the offered salary and our Total Compensation Calculator, https://inside.sou.edu/hrs/comp\-calc.html, demonstrates our value and commitment to our employees.
  
  

**POSITION DESCRIPTION:**  

Southern Oregon University (SOU) is looking for an experienced, analytical, and results oriented accountant to fill the position of Foundation Accounting Manager. This position is responsible for managing and overseeing the foundation's financial operations alongside a contracted fractional CFO. The primary focus of the position is on financial reporting, budgeting and forecasting, and accounting operations, including accounts payable and accounts receivable; month\-end posting procedures; reconciliations; audit preparation, and other day\-to\- day functions of accounting services.
  
  

The Accounting Manager must be able to successfully balance multiple priorities, provide excellent service to staff and donors, and demonstrate strong analytical skills. Further, the Accounting Manager will play a key role in analyzing trends, helping to improve systems and performance, and effectively implement policies and procedures to support the growth of the university's giving programs. The Accounting Manager must be able to set their own priorities to meet business demands, make decisions autonomously, and work collaboratively with stakeholders.
  
  

Minimum Requirements
  
  

BA/BS/BBA in Accounting or related field, plus minimum 2 years of related experience
  
Demonstrated experience with general ledger management and reconciliations.
  
A comprehensive understanding of accounting as a functional area, including accounts payable, accounts receivable, cash receipts, journal entries, reporting, reconciliations, posting procedures, and overall general ledger management.
  
Ability to understand fund management databases, due\-to/due\-from accounting, and fund balances.
  
Excellent computer skills and proficiency with a variety of computer applications including Microsoft Excel, word\-processing, spreadsheets, and databases.
  
Demonstrated skills in customer and service centered skills.
  
Knowledge of Generally Accepting Accounting Principles (GAAP).
  
  

Preferred Requirements
  
  

Non\-profit accounting practices (fund accounting).
  
Experience with The Financial Edge and Fundriver software
  
  

Essential Functions
  
  

Duties \- The following examples are typical work activities that are meant to illustrate the general range of work functions and are not meant to be all\-inclusive or restrictive:
  
  

(70%) Duties Include
  
  

Working alongside the foundation's fractional CFO, maintain overall quality, integrity and compliance of the Raiser's Edge and Fundriver databases\-\-including, but not limited to:
  
  

Applies complex accounting principles and procedures in working with specialized accounting records and reports. Assembles and analyzes data and transactions, prepares statements and reports relating to all foundation transactions.
  
Creates and reviews procedures for compliance, internal controls, reconciliations, and accounting practices. Supports the preparation of the SOU Foundation audit and financial statements.
  
Reviews, explains, interprets and executes policies and procedures on all aspects of financial data management.
  
Creates, reviews, and verifies transactions for accuracy and compliance and to ensure audit trail requirements are being met and procedures for recording information have been followed.
  
Prepares regular bank deposits, including completing electronic deposit of checks.
  
Completes regular reconciliations in accounts payable, accounts receivable, cash receipts, fund balances, banking, and investments. Identifies and resolves accounting errors or discrepancies.
  
Completes monthly posting procedures which include reconciliation and entry, fee assessment, distribution of investment earnings, posting of gift data.
  
Uses accounting records and supporting documentation to compile financial statements, budget recaps, and other special reports. Prepares reports for Foundation committees and board meetings.
  
  

(20%) Duties Include
  
  

Extract data via lists and reports to support various engagement activities across the university with accuracy and in a timely fashion. Including, but not limited to:
  
  

Develops complex queries/exports/reports to support the development staff, foundation affiliates and
  
university stakeholders
  
Presents data in the most meaningful way that is readily accessible, taking into consideration the level of knowledge of the requestor
  
Collaborates with end users to gather requirements and ensure proper testing and validation
  
Provides support/training to end users on running standardized and ad hoc reports
  
  

(10%) Other duties as assigned
  
  

Skills, Knowledge, and Abilities
  
  

Excellent communication skills; demonstrated ability to effectively communicate information in a clear and understandable manner, both verbally and in writing.
  
Demonstrated customer service experience requiring a very high level of diplomacy and professionalism to effectively handle a broad range of sensitive interpersonal situations.
  
Demonstrated ability to interpret and consistently apply a wide variety of complex policies and procedures where specific guidelines may not always exist.
  
Expressed ability to work with frequent changes in policies and procedures, under pressure of deadlines in a fast\-paced environment.
  
Demonstrated ability to proactively assess work operations and anticipate potential problems; ability to develop and implement strategies for preventing/resolving problems.
  
Great ability to effectively perform work of a highly sensitive and confidential nature that requires access to information. Must be able to exercise sound judgment and discretion, tact, and diplomacy.
  
Takes initiative in independently planning, organizing, and performing work assignments within broadly defined parameters
  
Demonstrated ability to work with a high level of productivity and accuracy/attention to detail.
  
Excellent organizational and time management skills with the demonstrated ability to set own priorities to coordinate multiple assignments with fluctuating and time\-sensitive deadlines.
  
Excellent computer skills and proficiency with a variety of computer applications including word processing, spreadsheets, databases, online systems, social media platforms, Internet as well as online calendaring and email.
  
Demonstrated ability to initiate, establish, and foster communication and teamwork by maintaining a positive, cooperative, productive work atmosphere in and outside the University with the ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships within a diverse population and with those from various cultural backgrounds.
  
Willingness to and work effectively in a heavily bureaucratic environment which requires regular interaction with a number of levels within the organization and multiple outside agencies.
  
Working knowledge or ability to quickly learn, university infrastructure, policies, and procedures.
  
Demonstrated skills in an institutional/educational environment utilizing a customer\-oriented and service\-centered attitude.
  
  

Physical Demands
  
  

Normal office activities such as sitting/standing at a desk, using a computer and communicating in person both in writing and via phone with others.
  
  

Special Conditions
  
  

Must be willing to travel and attend training programs off\-site for occasional professional development.
  
Must be able to work additional hours and adjust working hours to meet special jobs. May be called back periodically to perform work as needed on an emergency basis.
  
Must be able to successfully pass a pre\-employment background check.
  
This position classification has been defined as exempt and is not subject to the overtime provisions of the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA).
  
The person holding this position is considered a mandatory reporter under the Oregon Revised Statutes and is required to comply with the requirements set forth by the Oregon Department of Human Services.
  
This position is gift funded and renewal of appointment is contingent upon continued funding. When a position experiences a loss in funding, employees whose appointments are funded by that grant/gift may be terminated without notice.

**Diversity Statement:**  

Southern Oregon University is a welcoming community committed to inclusive excellence and the celebration of diversity. Without diversity, our educational process is diminished. Working together in support of our commitment to diversity, we strengthen and enrich our role as learners, educators, and members of a tightly connected global community. We encourage those who share in our commitment to diversity, to join our community and we expect all our employees to demonstrate an ability and desire to create an inclusive campus community.
  
  

SOU Land Acknowledgement
  
We want to take this moment to acknowledge that Southern Oregon University is located within the ancestral homelands of the Shasta, Takelma, and Latgawa peoples who lived here since time immemorial. These Tribes were displaced during rapid Euro\-American colonization, the Gold Rush, and armed conflict between 1851 and 1856\. In the 1850s, the discovery of gold and settlement brought thousands of Euro\-Americans to their lands, leading to warfare, epidemics, starvation, and villages being burned. In 1853 the first of several treaties were signed, confederating these Tribes and others together \- who would then be referred to as the Rogue River Tribe. These treaties ceded most of their homelands to the United States, and in return, they were guaranteed a permanent homeland reserved for them. At the end of the Rogue River Wars in 1856, these Tribes and many other Tribes from western Oregon were removed to the Siletz Reservation and the Grand Ronde Reservation. Today, the Confederated Tribes of Grand Ronde Community of Oregon ( www.grandronde.org ) and the Confederated Tribes of Siletz Indians ( www.ctsi.nsn.us ) are living descendants of the Takelma, Shasta, and Latgawa peoples of this area. We encourage YOU to learn about the land you reside on and to join us in advocating for the inherent sovereignty of Indigenous people.
  
  

Notice to Prospective Employees
  
Section 485 of the Higher Education Act, and The Federal Crime Awareness and Campus Security Act of 1990 (now referred to as the "Clery Act"), require that prospective employees be notified of the availability of SOU's Annual Security and Fire Safety Report. The report provides the annual statistics and campus policies for the reporting of and responding to campus crimes and fires; access to campus facilities; conduct code and campus policies on the use, possession, and sale of drugs/alcohol; and educational/information programs to inform the campus community about campus security procedures and crime prevention.
  
  

An electronic copy of the Annual Security Report (ASR) can be accessed at the following link: https://cps.sou.edu/clery\-act\-annual\-security\-report/ . A physical copy of the ASR is available at no charge upon request. To request a copy, please visit the Office of the General Counsel located in Churchill Hall, 1250 Siskiyou Boulevard, Ashland, Oregon 97520\. For more information, call 541\-552\-7095, or email clerycoordinator@sou.edu .
  
  

**To apply, please visit:** https://sou.wd1\.myworkdayjobs.com/en\-US/Southern\_Oregon\_University/job/Southern\-OregonAshland\-Campus/Foundation\-Accounting\-Manager\_R0000712

title: Comptroller (Remote – US Based)
description:
**ABOUT CFO LEVERAGE**

CFO Leverage is a fast\-growing fractional CFO and bookkeeping firm serving nonprofits exclusively. We partner with mission\-driven organizations across the United States — from charter schools and faith\-based ministries to social service agencies and advocacy groups — delivering financial clarity, compliance, and strategic guidance.

We operate as an embedded financial team for our clients. Our fractional CFOs own the client relationship at the strategic level, and our Comptrollers are the engine that keeps everything running accurately underneath them. This is a high\-trust, high\-ownership role on a team that takes its work seriously.

**THE ROLE**

We are looking for an experienced Comptroller to support a portfolio of nonprofit clients on a part\-time, fractional basis. You will sit between the bookkeeping team and the fractional CFO — owning the accuracy and integrity of the financials, ensuring compliance, and producing the reporting that drives client decisions.

This is not a data\-entry role. We need someone who can think critically about a set of books, identify issues before they become problems, and communicate with confidence to both internal team members and, when needed, client leadership.

**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES**

* Oversee and review the work of bookkeepers across a portfolio of nonprofit clients
* Ensure month\-end and year\-end close processes are completed accurately and on time
* Prepare and review financial statements: P\&L, Balance Sheet, Statement of Cash Flows, Budget vs. Actual
* Manage and enforce internal controls across client accounts, including payment approval workflows
* Ensure proper fund accounting — tracking restricted, unrestricted, and temporarily restricted funds
* Support audit preparation and serve as primary liaison with external auditors when needed
* Review and approve vendor payments and bill processing through Bill.com
* Maintain compliance with US GAAP and nonprofit accounting standards
* Identify and flag financial risks, discrepancies, or control weaknesses to the fractional CFO
* Train and support bookkeeping staff on best practices and client\-specific requirements
* Assist fractional CFOs with financial analysis, forecasting, and board reporting as needed

**REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS**

* 5\+ years of accounting experience, with at least 2 years in a Controller or Comptroller role
* Significant nonprofit accounting experience — you understand fund accounting, restricted grants, and Form 990
* Deep proficiency in QuickBooks Online
* Experience with Bill.com for payment processing and approval workflows
* Proven ability to manage and review the work of bookkeeping staff
* Experience supporting or managing external audits
* Excellent written and verbal communication — you can explain financials clearly to non\-financial audiences
* Highly organized, detail\-oriented, and reliable — clients are counting on you
* US\-based and authorized to work in the United States

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**

* Experience in a fractional, outsourced, or multi\-client accounting environment
* Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance
* Experience with nonprofit board reporting and financial presentations

**WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE DIFFERENT**

Most Comptroller roles put you in one organization, managing one set of books. At CFO Leverage, you will work across multiple nonprofit clients — which means variety, challenge, and the opportunity to build broad expertise quickly. You will work alongside experienced fractional CFOs, not in isolation. And you will have direct impact on organizations doing meaningful work in their communities.

We are growing fast. People who perform well here will have room to take on more — whether that means a larger client portfolio, a team lead role, or eventually stepping into a fractional CFO position.

**WHY CFO LEVERAGE**

* 100% remote — work from anywhere in the US
* Fractional / part\-time structure — flexibility without sacrificing meaningful work
* Stable, recurring client base — no feast\-or\-famine freelancing
* Collaborative team with experienced CFOs who value your expertise
* Mission\-driven clients — every organization you support is doing good in the world
* Clear growth path as the firm scales

**HOW TO APPLY**

Apply through Indeed with your resume and a cover letter. In your cover letter, please address:

* Your experience with nonprofit accounting and fund accounting specifically
* The size and complexity of the client portfolios or organizations you have managed
* Your QuickBooks Online and Bill.com proficiency levels
* Why a fractional / multi\-client role appeals to you

We read every application personally. Generic cover letters will not be considered — we are looking for people who understand what this role is and are genuinely excited about it.

Job Types: Full\-time, Part\-time

Pay: $55\.00 \- $70\.00 per hour

Expected hours: 15 – 30 per week

Work Location: Remote

title: Fractional CFO
description:
**Job Posting: Fractional Chief Financial Officer (CFO)**

**Location:** Las Olas, Fort Lauderdale, FL (Hybrid: 1–2 days on\-site weekly) **Commitment:** Fractional / Retainer basis **Firm:** Hernandez Development \& Construction

**The Mandate**

Hernandez Development \& Construction is a vertically integrated real estate powerhouse with 23 years of "Execution Certainty." As we manage a $2B\+ portfolio of Class A industrial and commercial assets, we are seeking a **Fractional CFO** who does more than report on the past—we need a visionary to architect our financial future.

This is a strategic role designed for a "Systems Architect" CFO. You will own our data, evaluate our financial infrastructure, assess our human capital within the finance department, and implement the technology necessary to scale toward our next billion in assets.

**Key Responsibilities1\. Data Sovereignty \& Financial Forecasting**

* **The Crystal Ball:** Move beyond historical reporting to build robust, predictive financial models and 13\-week cash flow forecasts.
* **Project Performance:** Establish deep\-dive data analytics for project\-level profitability, ensuring "Problem/Solution/Result" metrics are visible in real\-time.
* **Investor Reporting:** Elevate the sophistication of our financial reporting for capital partners and internal stakeholders.

**2\. Systems \& Infrastructure Architecture**

* **Tech Stack Optimization:** Audit our current financial tech stack (SAGE, Procore, etc.) and lead the implementation of automated systems that eliminate manual data entry and silos.
* **Scaling the "Hernandez Way":** Standardize SOPs for all financial workflows, ensuring the infrastructure can support rapid vertical growth without adding unnecessary headcount.

**3\. Team \& Talent Assessment**

* **Human Capital Audit:** Evaluate the current internal accounting/finance team. Identify skills gaps, mentor high\-potential staff, and advise on future department structure.
* **Accountability Frameworks:** Establish KPIs for the finance team to ensure alignment with the company’s "Purpose Driven" culture.

**4\. Capital \& Risk Strategy**

* **Lender/Partner Relations:** Act as the primary financial voice in high\-level negotiations with lenders and equity partners.
* **Work\-In\-Progress (WIP) Management:** Ensure absolute accuracy in WIP reporting and revenue recognition to safeguard the firm’s bonding capacity and credit\-worthiness.

**The Requirements**

* **The Track Record:** 15\+ years of financial leadership, with significant experience in **Commercial Real Estate (CRE)** or **General Contracting (GC)**.
* **Tech\-Forward:** You must be highly technology\-savvy. We are looking for someone who understands ERPS, data visualization tools (PowerBI/Tableau), and modern AEC fintech.
* **The "Builder" Mindset:** You enjoy the "cleanup and build" phase of a company’s evolution. You aren't just a consultant; you are an owner of the outcome.
* **Proximity:** Ability to be on\-site at our **Las Olas, Fort Lauderdale** headquarters 1–2 days per week to collaborate with the executive team.

Job Types: Part\-time, Contract, Temporary

Pay: $6,000\.00 \- $9,000\.00 per month

Work Location: In person

title: Controller
description:
**Controller** – Join our team in flexible, innovative work to make a direct impact on growing businesses.

**Logistics:** This role is open to remote employees in select US states: Colorado (Denver/Boulder), Texas (Austin/Dallas), Michigan (Detroit) and Utah (Salt Lake).

 **About AVL Growth Partners:**


AVL Growth Partners is a committed, Fractional Firm for rapidly growing small and medium sized enterprises. We step in as our clients’ dedicated finance and accounting team while they grow and scale with our expert guidance.

**The Who**:


Our unique offerings and client engagements require an exceptionally unique and handcrafted team.


Since the beginning, AVL has been a magnet for alternative thinkers, the entrepreneurial minded, and the doers that will stop at nothing to leave our clients better than how we found them.


As a team we have a shared commitment to live up to our name by stepping in as a growth partner for every client, and tackling each challenge associated with that process.


It takes more than good “talent” to make the difference. It takes a special team of ambitious, awesome nerds to do what we do as we execute our day to day with the chaos and complexity of rapidly growing businesses.

**What you will experience at AVL:**


At AVL, growth and exposure is the mindset.


On a daily basis our crew is exposed to various industries from consumer goods to SaaS, aerospace, technology, and architecture (to name a few).


Being on the AVL team means you get a seat at the table with some of the top emerging businesses in their respective fields.


It means you get an opportunity to gain experience being at the forefront of the process, working hands\-on and having a direct impact on growing businesses.


The daily life at AVL leads to growth and exposure on a whole new level.

**Position Overview:**


As a Controller at AVL you will provide work that directly impacts clients’ and their goals, by managing and reporting on the technical accounting systems to bring efficiency and visibility to their operations. The work you do on your client engagements is the vital foundation to allow your fellow Accountant and CFO team members to successfully lead the growth and expansion of our clients as a selected unit.


With the challenges of multiple clients and rapid growth from many angles, our Controllers are continually called upon to provide the expertise and support to manage their responsibilities in an experienced and professional manner.

**Who We Are Looking For?**


AVL is seeking a Controller with a proficient and demonstrated background in working with investor and venture\-backed companies with exposure and experience in various industries.


Our Controllers serve clients with both timely and proactive operational information in order to help them clearly understand the current state of their business.


You will possess an adept ability to translate “the numbers” into actionable information and you ensure the accounting operations are working efficiently and effectively with auditable internal controls. **You will be responsible for:**

* Providing the AVL leadership team and/or client executive management team qualitative and quantitative review of current financials on a monthly basis
* Preparing monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statement packages
* Maintaining, reviewing, and reporting on short\-term cash forecasts, long\-term financial pro\-forma forecasts, and actual results compared to budget/plan
* Assisting in preparation of annual budgets
* Advising on and ensuring compliance with tax, audit, legal, and other financial regulations (e.g. sales/use tax, property tax, employee benefits, payroll taxes, debt covenants, various insurances etc.)
* Overseeing the operational execution and accuracy and controls of invoicing, A/R collections, revenue reconciliations, balance sheet reconciliations, A/P procedures, and similar to ensure compliance with GAAP accounting standards
* Maintaining a system of internal controls over accounting transactions

**Experience Required:**

* A minimum of 10 years of total financial / accounting / business experience with 8 of those years being with accounting systems and accounting policies/procedures and at least 6 years at the Controller level in a progressive accounting environment
* Demonstrated experience at a high level working in industries such as consumer packaged goods, Tech, Saas, Services, to name a few
* BA/BS in Accounting or Business degree with an emphasis in Accounting
* CPA (active or inactive)
* A master of Excel
* Outstanding communication and management skills
* Working knowledge of Quickbooks, Bill.com, and/or other ERP systems
* Demonstrated working knowledge of IT systems and tools to benefit accounting operations
* Knowledge and understanding of inventory, Trade / Deductions, EComm Systems, Amazon (Fulfillment by Amazon), Shopify, Stripe

**Successful team members at AVL consistently demonstrate the following:**

* Ability to prioritize and manage multiple high priority engagements at once
* Strong communication and high emotional intelligence \- ability to navigate relationships
* Resourcefulness and willingness to learn and gain new information
* Comfortable working both independently and with a team
* Adaptable to change and comfortable with ambiguity
* Loves a good challenge and will naturally thrive in a changing environment
* Ability to create order and efficient processes from disarray
* Has a strong business acumen and can understand the big picture

**Compensation**


Compensation for this position is based on experience and technical proficiency. The hourly pay rate is between $55\.00\-$72\.00 per hour. This position offers optionality with the number of hours worked to be between 20\-35 hours/week. This position is eligible for incentive annual compensation in addition to base pay.

**Perks of working at AVL:**

* Paid Wellness Leave
* Optional Medical, Dental, Vision (depending on hours), HSA Match, Disability \& Life Insurance
* 401K Option
* Freedom and flexibility of an "independently together" work culture where our team has flexibility in their day to day while occasionally coming together for fun team meetings and social gatherings
* Growth and exposure to working with the best and brightest peers and clients in the industry

title: Chief Financial Officer (Fractional)
description:
Description:
**Be Part of Something Radically Different**


At Holon Health, we don’t do “business as usual.” We’re a trailblazing healthcare startup reimagining what it means to care—for our clients and our people. We provide whole\-person care to justice\-involved individuals living with Substance Use Disorder (SUD)—and we do it with deep compassion, community wisdom, and an unshakable belief in second chances.


Our approach throws out the playbook. We integrate medical, behavioral, and social care. We prioritize prevention and recovery. We treat clients like people, not checkboxes. And just as importantly, we know that our team can only care for others if they’re cared for themselves. That’s why we lead with flexibility, trust, and a culture built on well\-being, inclusion, and respect.

*If you’re energized by meaningful work and want to feel good about how you spend your days, read on.*

**Position Summary**


We’re looking for a hands\-on, high\-impact Fractional CFO to serve as a strategic architect for Holon Health. As our first\-ever CFO, you won’t just manage books; you will build the financial engine that powers our mission. While you will take full ownership of financial planning and analysis, your true directive will be using data\-driven insights to optimize every corner of the company, from clinical outcomes to growth strategy.


This is a fractional engagement with a structured time commitment to be defined collaboratively based on organizational needs and your availability. We’re looking for someone who can show up with full presence and real ownership within that scope. Not a consultant at arm’s length, but a genuine member of the leadership team.


If you’re a team\-first operator who loves solving problems and thrives in scrappy environments where you’re both strategist and doer, you’ll fit right in here.

**What You'll Do**

***Strategic Growth \& Data Leadership***

* Partner with the Growth team to fine\-tune our go\-to\-market strategy, using data to identify high\-yield payer segments and geographic opportunities
* Collaborate with Product Development to prioritize engineering and design efforts based on projected ROI and impact on patient outcomes
* Work alongside the Clinical team to lead Healthcare Economic and Outcomes Research (HEOR), proving the financial and social value of our care model to payers and partners
* Design innovative compensation and incentive plans that foster deep team alignment with Holon’s mission and performance goals
* Partner with People Operations to develop and optimize predictive staffing models, ensuring workforce planning is grounded in financial sustainability and mission\-aligned growth

***Operational Excellence \& Scalability***

* Use financial and operational data to help Practice Management improve efficiency, clinical quality, and regulatory compliance
* Own the overall financial strategy to drive sustainable growth, including building sophisticated financial models, forecasts, and cash planning
* Support the CEO and Board in fundraising efforts (debt and equity), providing the narrative and data needed for investor relations

***Revenue Cycle Management (RCM)***

* Take full accountability for the RCM lifecycle, leading our existing team to improve outcomes in eligibility, billing, collections, and payer performance
* Serve as the internal expert on health plan contracting and reimbursement economics to ensure we are billing efficiently and correctly
* Identify and resolve bottlenecks in claims, denials, and payer behavior through rigorous KPI tracking


Requirements:
**What Your Bring to the Table**

* Multidimensional expertise: deep knowledge of healthcare finance paired with an ability to apply that logic to non\-finance functions like Product and People
* Fluency in data as a language; you don’t just report numbers, you translate them into actionable strategy
* Startup grit: you’re comfortable building systems from scratch without a massive corporate back\-office
* Low ego, high impact: you build trust across departments and value mission over titles
* The ability to operate as both strategist and executor; you’re not afraid of spreadsheets, workflows, or messy processes
* A collaborative leadership style grounded in humility, curiosity, and genuine care for the team

**Education and Experience**

***Required***

* 10\+ years of progressive finance experience, with at least 5 years in senior leadership (CFO, VP Finance, or Head of Finance)
* Proven track record in healthcare finance and RCM, specifically within behavioral health or complex care settings
* Experience with multi\-site healthcare operations and scaling early\-stage startups
* MBA or CPA; experience leading HEOR or data\-driven compensation design
* Experience managing and developing teams, including setting clear goals and driving accountability
* Demonstrated ability to operate as both strategist and executor in fast\-moving environments
* Prior fractional, interim, or multi\-client CFO experience strongly preferred

***Preferred***

* MBA, CPA, or other advanced finance/accounting credential
* Experience in an early\-stage startup or high\-growth environment where you helped build functions from scratch
* Experience with behavioral health billing complexity (auths, coding, payer variability, denials)

**The Holon Way**


We know that fractional leaders do their best work when they’re treated as true partners, not vendors. Here’s what you can expect from us:

* Competitive compensation structured as hourly rate or monthly retainer based on scope and availability
* Equity participation reflecting your role as a founding leadership team member
* Direct access to the CEO and Board: your voice will carry real weight
* A flexible, remote\-first engagement that respects your time and other commitments
* A mission\-driven team that leads with integrity, moves with purpose, and has your back
* Room to shape the financial infrastructure of a company doing work that genuinely matters

**Our Commitment to DE\&I**


Diversity, equity, and inclusion aren’t buzzwords here: they’re the foundation. We embrace the full spectrum of human experience and are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. If you’re passionate about our mission but don’t meet every qualification, we still encourage you to apply.

**Ready to do work that matters—on a team that sees you?**


Apply now. Let’s build something extraordinary, together.

title: Chief Financial Officer
description:
**Chief Financial Officer (CFO)**

Lead and mentor a team of accountants to ultimately deliver world\-class accounting and advisory solutions for nonprofit organizations.

**Job Title:** Chief Financial Officer

**Classification:** Full\-time, exempt

**Workplace Type:** Hybrid (3 days in\-office required)

**Department:** Client Services

**Reporting To:** Partner

As a **Chief Financial Officer** for our rapidly growing company, you will oversee the day\-to\-day operations of a team of 7\-8 professionals, consisting of 4\-5 Accounting Associates and Senior Accounting Associates, 1 Accounting Manager, 1 Accounting Assistant, and up to 1 Accounting Intern. Our CFO teams are responsible for ensuring the accurate and timely delivery of a full suite of accounting and bookkeeping services to a wide range of nonprofit clients. We are looking for an intrinsically motivated problem\-solver who values transparency, inclusion, collaboration, and impact. At The Charity CFO, you will leverage your passion for accounting and the nonprofit sector to make a lasting impact for your clients.

In this role, you will have the autonomy to manage your team and clients as your own book of business. We take an entrepreneurial approach to leadership and team development, and we want our CFO's to build their teams as if they are an entrepreneur. It is expected that you will work hard and invest the time into building out your team and developing lasting client relationships.

**What You Would Be Helping Us With:**

The CFO has many responsibilities. They oversee and handle the day\-to\-day operations of a team of 7\-8 professionals, and they are responsible for serving 30\-40 clients. This requires that they have advanced accounting knowledge, comfortability with technology, and significant management experience. CFO responsibilities can be divided into two major categories: management responsibilities and client responsibilities.

Management Responsibilities:

* Lead staff meetings and training.
* Conduct weekly one\-on\-one check\-ins with all team members.
* Answer team member questions.
* Proactively track team\-wide tasks, projects, and upcoming deadlines to remove roadblocks and ensure that your team has the tools they need to complete each task correctly and on time.
* Ensure that your team is effectively communicating with clients; step\-in when necessary to answer client questions or de\-escalate concerns.
* Plan for team member PTO or other absences to ensure that client deliverables are still met.
* Review KPIs, including deadlines met, responsiveness to email correspondence, and client profitability. When KPIs are off track, take ownership and work proactively to get back on track.
* Conduct a monthly evaluation of all team members, providing direct and actionable feedback to ensure accountability and to enable long\-term growth.
* Regularly evaluate staff capacity and skill to determine the assignment of client responsibilities.
* Interview candidates for open positions.
* Onboard new employees and ensure their successful transition to the company.
* Facilitate training workshops for new hires.
* Facilitate team member engagement by hosting team events and activities.
* Participate in firm leadership activities and “face of the firm” events, as assigned.
* Seek out opportunities for professional development and ongoing education.
* Fill\-in for team member absences, as necessary.

Client Responsibilities:

* Review and analyze monthly financial reports for all assigned clients to evaluate the financial health of each organization.
* Provide support to clients by preparing annual budgets, cash flow forecasts, and other financial analyses.
* Present financial report presentations to client leadership and boards of directors.
* Periodically check\-in with clients to assess client satisfaction, address issues, and build long\-term relationships.
* Participate in new client onboarding meetings to understand their unique needs and to build customized accounting processes.
* Manage client expectations and scope of services to ensure that we are ultimately meeting their needs.
* Participate in internal ‘desk review’ debrief meetings to identify areas for improvement and to develop a game plan for cleaning\-up any accounting issues.
* Ensure that your team maintains appropriate accounting processes for all assigned clients. Proactively revise accounting processes as necessary.
* On an annual basis, prepare and present a custom assessment of each client’s financial health, leveraging our proprietary Nonprofit Blueprint model.
* Communicate annual fee increases with assigned clients.
* Serve as a CFO and strategic advisor for assigned clients. Provide clients with answers to their accounting questions and communicate proactively when accounting problems are identified.

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s in Accounting or Accounting\-related field
* 3\-5 years of experience in full\-cycle accounting or public accounting as an auditor or outsourced accountant
* 1\-2 years of nonprofit accounting experience, preferred
* 2\-4 years of successfully building and managing a team
* CPA license, preferred
* Proficiency with Microsoft Excel
* Proficiency with accounting software
* Excellent critical thinking skills
* Advanced accounting knowledge

**What Success Looks Like in the First 90 Days:**

Like we mentioned above, we’ll provide you with all the support and training you need to crush your goals inside the workplace. For this role, this means that within 90 days, we will prepare you to:

* Analyze financial statements and present key takeaways to stakeholders.
* Prepare budgets and forecasts for clients.
* Manage your team to ensure client needs are met and deliverables are consistently on time.
* Maintain accounting processes to ensure quality and to meet our clients’ unique needs
* Problem solve with clients, providing strategic advisory support and ‘out of the box’ solutions.
* Maintain client relationships for long\-term satisfaction and retention.
* Be an ambassador for The Charity CFO, externally and internally.

**Who is The Charity CFO, LLC?**

We provide premier accounting, advisory, and fractional CFO solutions for nonprofits. Our mission is to help nonprofit leaders by creating clarity and understanding of their organization’s financial position, modernizing financial operations, and bringing the focus back to what really matters \- the people and communities they serve. Our vision is to be the largest nonprofit accounting service firm in the United States.

**Our Culture \& Values**

At The Charity CFO, we put our people before revenue or clients, which has allowed us to create a unique workplace that values you first as a human being and then as an employee. We’ll provide you with all the support and training you need to crush your goals inside the workplace, along with the flexibility, benefits, and downtime to build a life you love outside the office.

Inside our doors, you’ll find a respectful, collaborative environment with team members with all levels of professional experience from beautifully diverse backgrounds. Everyone is welcome here, and you’ll be judged solely on your performance, regardless of any limitations society may have previously placed on you.

**Our Benefits**

From career advancement to free parking to 14 paid holidays, 4 weeks of PTO, and paid new parental leave, our comprehensive benefits package makes it easier to be there for your family, plan for the future and truly enjoy your downtime. Learn more about all of these benefits .

**How to Apply**

We want to acknowledge that imposter syndrome is real, so bullet points aside, if our mission, culture \& values resonate with you, we want to hear from you! If you are someone who is committed to connecting the dots, supporting clients and colleagues, and want to work in a position that allows you to develop your accounting experience on work that really matters, please apply on our .

If you require special accommodations during the recruitment process, please email our recruiting team whose contact information can be found at the bottom of our .

*The Charity CFO, LLC is an equal\-opportunity employer and we value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, dietary preferences, gender, sexual orientation, age, fashion sense, marital status, veteran status, disability status, political leanings, or any other category protected by applicable federal, state or local laws.*

**Additional Notices**

*Employment at The Charity CFO is at\-will. This job description is not an exhaustive list of all functions that may be required, and The Charity CFO reserves the right to require additional functions as well as to revise this job description at any time.*

title: Accounting Manager - Nonprofit
description:
**Job Overview**  
The ideal candidate will possess extensive experience in governmental and public accounting, with proficiency in various accounting software and financial analysis. As a key member of our team, you will ensure accurate financial reporting, effective cash management, and adherence to GAAP and other regulatory standards to support the organization’s mission\-driven objectives.

**Position Summary**

We are seeking a highly skilled and detail\-oriented Accounting Manager to join our nonprofit team and serve as the primary, hands\-on accounting professional to oversee the financial operations of our organization. This role requires a strong understanding of nonprofit accounting principles, grant management, regulatory compliance, financial reporting and overall financial operations. The ideal candidate will be proactive, organized, and detail\-oriented fast\-learner that is motivated to grow quickly into a Senior Financial Leadership role.

This role will work closely with organizational leadership and receive guidance from current Interim CFO. It provides a clear pathway to quickly advance into a Controller, Director of Finance, or Chief Financial Officer role, supported through mentorship and professional development.

**Key Responsibilities**

Accounting Operations

* Manage daily accounting activities, including accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll entries, journal entries, and general ledger maintenance.
* Perform month\-end close, bank reconciliations, balance sheet reconciliations, accruals, and depreciation schedules.
* Prepare and analyze monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements.
* Ensure compliance with GAAP, nonprofit accounting standards, and internal controls.
* Administer payroll and payroll tax functions.
* Maintain proper documentation and support organizational financial policies and procedures.
* Work closely with leadership and program managers to provide financial guidance and operational insight.

Grant Accounting \& Compliance

* Track, manage, and report on all federal, state, and private grant budgets and expenditures.
* Prepare grant budgets, drawdowns, financial reports, invoices, and funder\-specific documentation.
* Ensure compliance with federal Uniform Guidance (2 CFR 200\), state regulations, and grantor requirements.
* Monitor grant spend vs. budget and ensure accurate allocation of direct and indirect costs.
* Serve as a financial liaison with funders and grant partners as needed.

Financial Reporting

* Prepare monthly internal financial reports for leadership.
* Support quarterly and annual reporting packages for the Board of Directors.
* Analyze variances and provide explanations to leadership and program teams.

Budgeting \& Forecasting

* Assist the Fractional CFO with annual budget development.
* Provide ongoing forecasting and spending analyses for grants and organizational programs.
* Support program managers in understanding and managing their budgets.

Audit \& External Reporting

* Prepare year\-end audit schedules and documentation.
* Support financial audits
* Assist in Form 990 preparation and compliance filings.

Cash Management

* Monitor cash flow, prepare cash reconciliations, and identify financial risks.
* Process vendor payments, credit card activity, and expense reimbursements.
* Oversee purchasing controls and ensure timely payment processes.

Systems \& Process Improvement

* Maintain the accounting system and recommend workflow improvements.
* Identify opportunities for automation suitable for a one\-person department.
* Strengthen and refine internal controls and financial procedures.

Leadership \& Growth

* Serve as the organization’s primary accounting resource and internal advisor.
* Partner with leadership on strategy, operations, and financial decision\-making.
* Opportunity to grow into a Controller, Director of Finance, or CFO role as the organization expands.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (CPA or master's preferred but not required).
* 4–7\+ years of progressive accounting experience, including nonprofit finance experience
* Strong knowledge of nonprofit fund accounting, grant compliance, and financial reporting.
* Experience with federal/state grant management, including drawdowns.
* Proficient in accounting software; QuickBooks preferred
* Advanced Excel skills and exceptional attention to detail.
* Experience with payroll processing and benefits administration.
* Strong analytical, communication, and problem\-solving skills.
* Ability to work independently, manage multiple deadlines, and collaborate across teams.

We encourage all interested applicants to apply, even if your experience doesn’t perfectly align with every qualification listed. If the role or title is not an exact match, we welcome the opportunity to discuss how your skills may fit within our growing organization.

Pay: $70,000\.00 \- $95,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k) matching
* 403(b) matching
* Dental insurance
* Disability insurance
* Employee assistance program
* Health insurance
* Paid sick time
* Paid time off
* Retirement plan
* Vision insurance

* Do you have non\-profit accounting experience?
